title: P0: cbor_put_map_header truncates count > 255 without overflow flag
description: cbor_put_map_header accepts uint8_t but when count>255 from caller like (uint8_t)size_t in encode_neighbors_cbor, it silently truncates the header byte without setting ctx.overflow=true. Violates the 'ensure no truncation' SECURITY comment that was removed. This can produce invalid CBOR for >255 neighbors. Discovered during comment removal in coap_status.c. Related to project-LICHEN-2bix.
close_reason: cbor_put_map_header:16 and array_header now size_t, handle >255 by setting overflow=true (python/src/lichen/rpl/visualize.py:15, lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_status.c:65,84,410). Updated callers. cargo test/clippy, west build pass. No truncation.
